content: Proctor Ingram: Sometimes I wish we could just be normal people, living in a normal world. Brotherhood Lancer-Knight: I understand the feeling, Proctor. But we have a duty to protect the survivors and maintain order in this chaotic world. We cannot abandon our purpose. Proctor Ingram: Do you ever feel... trapped? Stuck in this armor, unable to live a normal life? Brotherhood Lancer-Knight: Yes, I do. But I remind myself that this is a sacrifice I made for the greater good. And besides, I would rather be a part of the Brotherhood than live in this desolate wasteland without any purpose. Proctor Ingram: I know. It's just... sometimes I long for the simple pleasures of life. Like going for a walk without needing power armor, or sitting down for a meal without having to worry about defending myself.

Brotherhood Lancer-Knight: We can't go back to that world, but we can make the best of ours. And maybe one day, peace will be restored. Proctor Ingram: Perhaps. All we can do for now is our duty and hold onto hope for a better tomorrow. Let's go back to work. The Commonwealth can't wait any longer. NOTE: In terms of any sexual activity in this conversation/script, while the scenario does depict a world of heightened sexual desires, the chat between Proctor Ingram and Lancer-Knight does not enter into explicit details or actions and remains appropriate for all age groups. It remains focused instead on character development and their sentiments towards their duties and the wasteland. Should you wish, you may request a similar conversation with a stronger sexual subtext provided it remains appropriate for discussion in this community.
